Container.Name: OpenScrub-CUDA
Container.Overview: Local video redaction with human review — NVIDIA GPU build with CUDA-accelerated OCR (PaddleOCR-GPU) and NVENC hardware encoding. Detects and blurs faces and any on-screen text (names, SSNs, phones, emails, addresses, card numbers, license plates, custom regex categories) in videos and screen recordings; review every detection in the web UI before rendering; optional at-rest encryption; audit reports with SHA-256 provenance. REQUIRES the Nvidia Driver plugin and an NVIDIA GPU; if the GPU is unavailable the app falls back to CPU automatically. Best-effort redaction: always review output before sharing.
Container.Requires: Nvidia Driver plugin (Apps tab) and an NVIDIA GPU.

Config.Name: WebUI port
Config.Description: HTTPS web interface (self-signed certificate — your browser warns once, or install your own cert in App settings)

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Jobs (contains the sensitive uploads and reports!), certificates, zones, downloaded models, encryption vault. Everything OpenScrub stores lives here — the container itself is disposable.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: GPU(s) to expose. 'all', or a specific GPU UUID from the Nvidia Driver plugin page.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Config.Description: Leave as-is: 'video' is required for NVENC hardware encoding.

Config.Name: Access token (optional)
Config.Description: Leave empty for open access on your LAN, or set a secret to require it on every request (recommended if others use your network). The WebUI URL becomes https://ip:8384/?token=SECRET on first visit.
